heroHeadline: BIA Rules DACA Status Can No Longer Block Deportation; 150+ 'Communities Not Cages' Protests in 33 States; ICE Arrests Down 11.7% Since Minneapolis
heroSubtitle: The Board of Immigration Appeals issued a landmark April 25, 2026 precedent ruling that DACA status alone is insufficient to halt removal proceedings — threatening protections for ~500,000 active Dreamers. The BIA backed government lawyers in 97% of published decisions in the prior year. Simultaneously, 150+ 'Communities Not Cages' rallies took place in 33 states against DHS's $38B warehouse detention expansion plan. An AP analysis of UC Berkeley data shows ICE weekly arrests fell 11.7% (8,347 → 7,369/week) after the January 2026 Minneapolis killings and agent pullback — though surging in Kentucky, Indiana, North Carolina, and Florida. DHS also quietly directed ICE field offices to stop entering homes without judicial warrants, per NBC News. FY2026 ICE custody deaths stand at 29 — a 22-year record.
footerNote: Data sourced from DHS, ICE official records, ACLU, HRW, DHS OIG, TRAC Reports, Detention Watch Network, court records, and Congressional records
